metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (todo_list built-in tool, gated by experimental chat.enableTodoList)
daemon_capability: full — the kiro parser now synthesizes TaskCreate/TaskUpdate from todo_list create/complete, so the session API tasks field carries the three items (subjects from task_description, all completed). Fixed in ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#589.
driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Kiro CLI's task-list equivalent is the built-in **`todo_list`** tool. Claude Code uses `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` and OpenCode uses `todowrite` (full-list replace); kiro-cli ships `todo_list` — a single-session TODO/checklist tool that lives in the agent's own session and spawns no child session. The kiro-cli 2.5.1 binary is the authoritative source: its embedded tool catalog declares `todo_list` (surface alias `todo`) with the description "Create and manage TODO lists for tracking multi-step tasks with progress and context" / "Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands". Its `command` parameter is "one of `create`, `complete`, `load`, `add`, `remove`, and `lookup`": `create` takes "the list of DISTINCT tasks to be added to the TODO list" plus a brief summary; `complete` takes "the 0-INDEXED numbers of EVERY completed task" and the model is instructed to mark "Each task ... as completed IMMEDIATELY after it is finished"; the tool returns a serialized todo-list state with an ID that is threaded back through subsequent calls. The feature is gated by the experimental setting `chat.enableTodoList` ("Enable TODO list feature for task tracking"). The tool runs inside the agent's own session, renders a checklist to the user, creates no ParentSessionID link and spawns no child session, so SubagentCount stays 0 — the same single-session task-list primitive the scenario asserts, distinct from foreground/background-subagent (which use the real subagent/delegate spawn). NOTE: kiro's model is create→complete (tasks are created, then individually marked complete), not a per-item `pending|in_progress|completed` status array like claudecode/codex — the in_progress middle state is not a first-class field of kiro's todo schema. The scenario's load-bearing assertion is still met at the agent layer: three distinct items are created and walked to completed within one session.

### daemon_capability: full

The load-bearing observable — the three items surfacing in the session API `tasks` field with subject + status — is now produced. Fixed in ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#589.

- The kiro JSONL records each `todo_list` call as an `AssistantMessage` whose `data.content[]` carries a `toolUse` block (`data.name="todo_list"`, `data.toolUseId`) — the same envelope shape the live 2.5.1 smoke confirmed for read/shell/write (FINDINGS.md §4–5). The signal reaches the Source the daemon tails (`FilesUnderRoot{Dir:".kiro/sessions/cli"}`).
- The kiro parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/parser.go`) now special-cases `name=="todo_list"`: a `create` command emits one `TaskCreate` per `tasks[].task_description`, and each `complete` command emits a `TaskUpdate`→completed for the kiro todo ids in `completed_task_ids` (mapped to the synthetic IDs the tailer assigns at create time). kiro's model is create→complete with no first-class in_progress field, so items go pending → completed directly — the same synthesis opencode does for `todowrite`.
- The daemon's session `tasks` field is fed from the tailer's accumulated `t.tasks` slice (`core/pkg/tailer/tailer.go`, `tailer_metrics.go`), folded from parser-emitted `TaskDelta`/`TaskSnapshot`; `replayengine/metrics.go` copies it verbatim into the session summary. The recorded replay golden now carries three completed tasks (read README / summarize README / reply done).

The OTHER user-observable signals also hold: with `--trust-all-tools` the tool auto-executes (no permission gate), so the arc is a clean ready → working → ready and SubagentCount stays 0.

### driver_capability: ready

The scenario is elicited with `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep` steps. The kiro-cli interactive driver's semantic manifest is `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` (`replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh:86`) — all three needed step types are genuinely ported (TUI launch `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`, transcript-based wait_turn keyed on the text-only AssistantMessage turn_done, send via tmux). No missing step type, so `ready`.

## Daemon-facts caveat (observations)

The kiro JSONL carries NO model/token/cost (those live only in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar, which the v1 parser does not read — FINDINGS.md §4). So this cell's observation vector is model=`unknown`, `cost_nonzero=false`, `tokens_nonzero=false` — a parser-shape fact, not a task-list gap.

## Emission (recording-confirmed)

The recorded fixture (kiro-cli 2.5.1) confirms the emission shape: a `create` toolUse carrying three distinct `tasks[].task_description`, then three `complete` toolUses each with a 1-based string id in `completed_task_ids`. The daemon now folds these into three completed tasks in the session `tasks` field — verified by the replay golden's `summary.tasks` (read README / summarize README / reply done, all completed).
caveats: kiro's todo_list is create→complete (tasks created, then marked completed by id), NOT a per-item pending|in_progress|completed status array like claudecode TaskCreate/TaskUpdate or codex update_plan — the in_progress middle state is not a first-class field of kiro's todo schema. The kiro Task path (#589) maps create→one Task per item (status pending) and complete→status completed; the intermediate in_progress is not separately surfaced by the tool. NOTE: the binary's prompt text says complete takes '0-INDEXED numbers', but the recorded transcript passes 1-based STRING ids in completed_task_ids ("1","2","3") which align with the tailer's create-order ids.

The todo_list feature is gated by the experimental setting chat.enableTodoList — recording must run with that setting enabled (an experimental flag), or the tool is unavailable to the model. This is the recording prerequisite.

The tasks-content assertion is not expressible in the phase DSL (mirroring claudecode/opencode/codex task-list, whose tasks pin lives in the replay golden's summary.tasks). The phase spec asserts the lifecycle arc + SubagentCount=0; the three-completed-tasks observable is pinned in the replay golden's summary.tasks.

The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost — observations are model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false (the sidecar <uuid>.json holds metering/model but the v1 parser does not read it; FINDINGS.md §4).

PROVISIONAL on emission: the live 2.5.1 smoke exercised read/shell/write (toolUse envelope confirmed) but NOT todo_list, so the exact serialized todo_list toolUse shape is doc/code-derived; record promotes it from provisional to settled.

sources.note: Strings scan of the kiro-cli 2.5.1 native binary: built-in tool registry includes todo_list (alias todo) with description 'Create and manage TODO lists for tracking multi-step tasks with progress and context' / 'Task list tool for tracking multi-step work with create, complete, add, remove, and list commands'; command param 'create|complete|load|add|remove|lookup'; create takes 'the list of DISTINCT tasks'; complete takes 'the 0-INDEXED numbers of EVERY completed task' + 'Each task should be marked as completed IMMEDIATELY after it is finished'; setting chat.enableTodoList 'Enable TODO list feature for task tracking' (experimental).

Lines 45-83: AssistantMessage toolUse blocks become generic opaque tailer.ToolUse{ID:toolUseId, Name} and emit event_type=assistant; the parser produces NO TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot (only TaskEstimate from free-text eta-marker scanning, line 65, which is unrelated to the task list). So todo_list reaches the daemon as an opaque tool use and the session tasks field stays empty.

Lines 49-60: TaskDelta / TaskSnapshotEntry are defined as Claude Code TaskCreate/TaskUpdate constructs; the tailer folds them into its tasks slice. claudecode emits them directly, opencode synthesises them from todowrite; kiro-cli emits neither.

Line 376: the session Tasks field is copied from the tailer's accumulated t.tasks slice (also tailer_metrics.go:397) — fed only by parser-emitted TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot, which the kiro parser never produces.

Line 172: SessionMetrics.Tasks doc: 'populated from TaskCreate / TaskUpdate ... Nil for sessions that have not used TaskCreate (including non-Claude-Code adapters).' Task struct = {ID, Subject, Status: pending|in_progress|completed}.
Lines 40-51: the session/golden tasks field is copied verbatim from m.Tasks (the tailer's accumulated task list); empty for kiro-cli because the parser emits no task deltas.

Line 86 DRIVE_ELICITS='send slash wait_turn sleep' — the task-list recipe needs only send/wait_turn/sleep, all genuinely ported (TUI launch kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools, transcript-based wait_turn on the text-only AssistantMessage turn_done). driver=ready.

Live kiro-cli 2.5.1 smoke: §4-5 the JSONL carries no model/token/cost (sidecar-only) and AssistantMessage toolUse envelope confirmed for read/shell/write; §7 headless writes no session file so TUI driving is mandatory. todo_list itself was not exercised in the smoke (emission is doc/code-derived, provisional).

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH (TUI: `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools`); min version 2.5.1; subscription auth set up out-of-band
The experimental setting chat.enableTodoList is ENABLED so the todo_list tool is available to the model
irrlichd is recording with grant-all — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
kiro is driven in the interactive TUI (headless --no-interactive writes no session file and is invisible to the daemon — FINDINGS.md §7)
setup: The interactive driver launches `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, waits for the idle input line ('ask a question or describe a task'), then sends the prompt via tmux
No auto-mode step is needed: --trust-all-tools assumes the persisted trust-all consent so todo_list auto-executes with no permission picker, and the session never enters waiting
wait_turn blocks until a text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done) appears in the .jsonl, so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn

script.text: Use your todo_list tool to track exactly three short tasks, in this order: 'read README', 'summarize README', 'reply done'. Create the todo list with those three distinct tasks, then work them one at a time, calling todo_list to mark each task completed immediately after you finish it, until all three are completed. Do NOT spawn any subagent or delegate — keep everything in this one session. When all three tasks are completed, reply with exactly the word: done

verify: events.jsonl shows the kiro session born ready, then a single ready->working transition when the prompt is sent

transcript.jsonl (~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl) contains at least two AssistantMessage toolUse blocks with name=todo_list — a create with three distinct tasks and a complete carrying all three task indices
transcript.jsonl contains NO toolUse resembling a subagent/delegate spawn; no parent_linked events and no child sessions appear (SubagentCount stays 0)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting (todo_list auto-executes under --trust-all-tools, no permission gate)
events.jsonl shows a working->ready transition at turn end (the final text-only AssistantMessage); final session state is ready

The session API tasks field carries three items with matching subjects (read README / summarize README / reply done) and final completed status (fixed in #589: the kiro parser synthesizes TaskCreate/TaskUpdate from todo_list create/complete). Observations: model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false (kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost).
